title: Funimations Eva DVDs and Blurays
wikitext:
[]: Funimation's Eva DVDs and Blurays             Quote Post by Clover » Fri Dec 10, 2010 5:23 pm I'm curious of the overall quality of the U.S. Funimation Blu-ray release of Evangelion 1.11. Many people hate Funimation, and for good reason, but does this release show the same quality as the R2 release? Does it have the unskipable trailers before the menu? Are the credits in English, instead of maintaining the normal credits with the streaming light effect? Does it say "tsuzuku" instead of "to be continued"? I don't have a Blu-ray player, but I want to know if it is worth buying, or if fansubbers really do do a better job than companies.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Dec 10, 2010 10:03 pm For what it's worth I have the 1.11 DVD (and 1.01 DVD) and I can confirm the following: Opening Title Credits, To Be Continued Credits, End Credits, and Next Time Preview Text/the upcoming episode text are ALL in English. Basically this means any instances of the Japanese Evangelion Shin Gekijōban: Jo (or Ha for the Next Time Preview) are replaced with Evangelion 1.0 You are (not) alone (or Evangelion 2.0 You can (not) advance for the Next Time Preview) in English. I was very surprised that they removed/replaced the "To Be Continued" and the "Next Time" Japanese texts for English versions, I thought for sure those would be subtitled or at least left in-tact (similar to say, ADV's Platinum DVDs). The lack of the Japanese rainbow credits, even as an extra, was a disappointment. It's just standard credits in English instead. Another disappointment for the 1.11 DVD is there is it only has 1 version each of the TV Spot/trailers/etc instead of including ALL the trailers/TV spots/etc that the 1.11 Blu-Ray at least has. (For example: You only get the very first trailer for 1.0 that features Beautiful World, not the other versions nor Fly Me To the Moon trailers.) If this was a space issue, I don't understand why considering the 1.11 DVD is a two-disc release . I'm a completest when it comes to things like this and I don't have the time or money to yet hop on the Blu-Ray Bandwagon, so this was a major annoyance on my end. Like I said here: Sailor Star Dust wrote: I just realized: Funimation's 1.11 DVD doesn't have much in the way of the trailers as extras (eg: No FMtTM trailers or the notice that specifically said "New machine, new character, new story"). I feel ripped-off they expect double-dipping for 1.11 BD, unless they're following Japan's releases to the letter. Why Funimation couldn't retain the Japanese credits or unedited Next-Time Preview (screen of English text instead of the usual subs and the Japanese text?! But ADV's Platinum was fine...) though... >_>

[]:             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Fri Dec 10, 2010 10:10 pm i'm a special features freak, but I think it was more or less that Funi didn't really give a shit about compiling all of the promotional 1.0/1.01/1.11 trailers/teasers/TV spots unlike Khara's Japanese DVD/Blu-Ray i've seen multiple versions of DVDs where one DVD would have a unique trailer that is nowhere to be seen on another and in some cases, one DVD edition has the trailers and then a special edition comes out yet forgets to put in the trailers and whatnot don't ask why, this stupid shit happens all the time :P and yeah, the lack of the actual end credits with the rainbow is a disappointment (since they'll remove the one for 2.22 as well)

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Fri Dec 10, 2010 11:06 pm Looks like I'm never buying domestic. I need to finish my Japanese language training.

[]:             Quote Post by Electric Sachiel » Fri Dec 10, 2010 11:24 pm GasmaskAvenger wrote: i'm a special features freak, but I think it was more or less that Funi didn't really give a shit about compiling all of the promotional 1.0/1.01/1.11 trailers/teasers/TV spots unlike Khara's Japanese DVD/Blu-Ray

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Dec 10, 2010 11:55 pm Electric Sachiel wrote: I thought they did compile most of those trailers and extra bonus features from the Japanese releases for funi's Eva 1.11 release.

[]:             Quote Post by COACH » Sat Dec 11, 2010 12:48 am Yeah, FUNimation doesn't do alternate angles anymore (they did for the longest time, even though most other anime companies just used their own English credits). They said it wastes space on the disks. Yeah, it kind of sucks, if you like your DVDs to match the Japanese release, but is it really that big of a deal? It's like complaining about an English release of a manga replacing all of the Japanese text with English... Hey, if it makes you wanna go buy the R2, more power to you, but isn't saying "The fansubbers did it better" just a little ridiculous?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Dec 11, 2010 1:06 am COACH wrote: Hey, if it makes you wanna go buy the R2, more power to you, but isn't saying "The fansubbers did it better" just a little ridiculous?

[]:             Quote Post by COACH » Sat Dec 11, 2010 1:20 am Sailor Star Dust wrote: Never said that and I think Fansubbers can suck just as much as professional companies, context depending.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva 04 » Sat Dec 11, 2010 2:50 am COACH wrote: Sorry, that was meant to be a reply to this: I should've made that more clear. ^.^ Yeah, I agree that it sucks when they leave stuff out (especially when you've said that the Blu-ray has stuff that your DVD doesn't), but when you've got people like the topic creator, who bash R1 companies for things like different menus, credits, title cards, etc., I just shake my head. Yeah, the Japanese credits were neat, but is it the end of the world?

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Sat Dec 11, 2010 9:34 am ...

[]:             Quote Post by bladerj » Sat Dec 11, 2010 10:09 am I was going to open a thread but since this is here, do you think 3.0 and 4.0 will have dvd releases ? dvd is pretty much on it's dying days.

[]:             Quote Post by Baz » Sat Dec 11, 2010 10:45 am bladerj wrote: I was going to open a thread but since this is here, do you think 3.0 and 4.0 will have dvd releases ? dvd is pretty much on it's dying days.

[]:             Quote Post by Dr. Nick » Sat Dec 11, 2010 10:51 am Seele00TextOnly wrote: The rainbow credits in the Rebuild films as well as the DNA-like helix credits in the middle of EoE are some of the most beautiful closing credits I've ever seen. They're art, and a part of the work. Just cutting them away (which thankfully even Manga Entertainment had the good sense not to do) is a senseless travesty

[]:             Quote Post by COACH » Sat Dec 11, 2010 10:52 am Seele00TextOnly wrote: The rainbow credits in the Rebuild films as well as the DNA-like helix credits in the middle of EoE are some of the most beautiful closing credits I've ever seen. They're art, and a part of the work. Just cutting them away (which thankfully even Manga Entertainment had the good sense not to do) is a senseless travesty; there's no reason, time constraints, anything. explaining away doing it. They didn't have to, and it's fair for some people to be miffed about it. If you don't care at all, fine; but there's hardly reason to be so dismissive of those that do care.

[]:             Quote Post by Baz » Sat Dec 11, 2010 10:59 am COACH wrote: It's like refusing to buy VIZ's translation of the manga, just because the text is all replaced with English, and there's no dust jacket!

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Sat Dec 11, 2010 11:14 am Funimation's credits are hideous, they scroll like they were created in 10 minutes, and the font is pig disgusting. Also, the "tsuzuku" is part of the movie. It's like going back and changing all of the Japanese text on the walls in the film to English. It's just stupid, and if you need to do it, put a subtitle at the bottom that can be disabled in the menu, like they did with some of their other DVDs.

[]:             Quote Post by Da Games Elite » Sat Dec 11, 2010 11:17 am Uh...I'm sorry, but aren't a few of you over reacting? Sure, here or there one thing will need to change, and maybe it's a tad annoying that it isn't replicated perfectly, but, even so...I think it may be a stretch to say that the entire DVD is ruined forever due to a change like that. Numerous other dubs get far worse treatment, but still, it isn't THAT large of a deal...

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Sat Dec 11, 2010 11:32 am ...

[]:             Quote Post by Hatsumi92 » Sat Dec 11, 2010 11:36 am Clover wrote: Funimation's credits are hideous, they scroll like they were created in 10 minutes, and the font is pig disgusting. Also, the "tsuzuku" is part of the movie. It's like going back and changing all of the Japanese text on the walls in the film to English. It's just stupid, and if you need to do it, put a subtitle at the bottom that can be disabled in the menu, like they did with some of their other DVDs.
